english: (t'a-mál (tha mal) abbreviated from t'á-ma-la (tha ma la)) 1. mean, vulgar, plebeian, t'a-mál-par °dúg-pa (tha mal par 'dug pa) to live like the vulgar Dzl. — 2. ordinary, usual, t'a-mál-pa ma yin (tha mal pa ma yin) that is no usual thing Dzl.; t'a-mál (tha mal) adv. = p'al-č'ér (phal cher).
Sch. 'oath'; but in two passages of Dzl. čii t'a-tsig (ci'i tha tshig) can only mean: 'what signifies?'
W. wide asunder, wide, *t'a-ra-t'o-ré żág-pa* (*tha ra tho re zhag pa*) C. to scatter, to throw loosely about.
1. Sch.: 'the breadth of a plain'. — 2. a medicinal herb Med., in Lh. Plantago major.
Tar. 20, 17, Schf.: 'the utmost limits', or it may be a p. n.
W., *t'e-li* (*the li*) C., Hind. थालिया, a tin plate.
W. a sort of red cloth.
Sch.: 'the end, the consequence; bad'; Bhar.: skyés-bu t'a-šál ñid (skyes bu tha shal nyid) Schf.: homo nequam, a good-for-naught.

1. sometimes for °t'ag ('thag), Glr. — 2. distance a. relatively (prob. from t'ág-pa (thag pa) measuring-cord, surveyor's chain) only in: t'ag-riṅ-ba (thag ring ba) adj. and abstract noun, t'ag-riṅ(-po) (thag ring (-po)) adj., W. *t'ag-riṅ-(mo)* (*thag ring -(mo)*) distant, a great distance, sa t'ag-riṅ(s) (sa thag ring(s)) a far country Glr.; with daṅ (dang) or las (las) far from; t'ag - mi - riṅ - ba (thag mi ring ba) not far Pth.; t'ag-riṅ(-po)-nas (thag ring (-po) nas) from afar, from a distance Thgy.; t'ag-ñé-ba (thag nye ba) near; proximity; W. adj. *t'ag-ñé-mo* (*thag nye mo*); t'ag či-tsam (thag ci tsam) how far? Cs.; t'ag-grú (thag gru) v. t'a-gru (tha gru). b. absolutely, only with respect to time, in: ma-t'ág (ma thag) but just, just now, gen. with a verbal root, sleb ma-t'ág yin-pa (sleb ma thag yin pa) he that has arrived just now Glr.; sṅar bšad ma-t'ág-pa (sngar bshad ma thag pa) (the passages) that have been explained just now Gram.; as an adv. gen. ma-t'ág-tu (ma thag tu), or only ma-t'ág (ma thag), frq., e.g. t'os ma-t'ág-tu (thos ma thag tu) as soon as he had heard; de ma-t'ág-tu (de ma thag tu) directly, immediately, in W. *ma-t'óg-tse* (*ma thog tse*). — 3. t'ag-t'óg (thag thog) v. tog-t'ág (thog thag). — 4. t'ag-gčód-pa (thag gcod pa) v. t'ág-pa (thag pa) I.
with *jhé'-pa* (*jhe' pa*) C., *č'o'-č'e'* (*cho' che'*) to knock, sgo (sgo) at the door.

I. rope, cord (in Lh. hempen ropes, as a foreign manufacture, are often distinguished from other ropes, by being called रसी, bal-t'ág (bal thag) rope made of wool, ral-t'ág (ral thag) rope of goat's hair, rtsid-t'ág (rtsid thag) rope of the long hair of the yak, rtsa-t'ág (rtsa thag), or pon-t'ág (pon thag) rope of grass; lčágs-(kyi) t'ág-pa (lcags (kyi) thag pa) chain, wire-rope, used as fetters or otherwise; *ras-t'ág* (*ras thag*) W. bandage; t'ag-míg (thag mig) mesh of a net Sch.; t'ag-zó (thag zo) rope-maker's work Pth. — t'ag gčód-pa (thag gcod pa) vb. a. (t'ag čód-pa (thag chod pa), or čád-pa (chad pa) vb. n. or pass.) 1. to cut a cord, bdag ñé-du daṅ °brél-t'ag bčád-pas bde (bdag nye du dang 'brel thag bcad pas bde) I am glad of having cut the cord (tie) which united me with my family Mil.; gen. with re (re), the cord of hope, e.g. °gró-bai ré-t'ag čad ('gro ba'i re thag chad) the cord of the hope of going on a journey is cut off, i.e. the journey has been given up Glr.; Schr.: °ó-t'ag gčód-pa ('o thag gcod pa) to wean (a child); bló-t'ag-čod (blo thag chod) deliberation is cut off, the matter is decided or resolved upon; hence frq. without blo (blo): 2. to decide, resolve, determine, rgyal-po bkroṅ-bar t'ag-bčád (rgyal po bkrong bar thag bcad) it was determined to murder the king Glr.; k'yod gñis ṅá-la čuṅ-ma mi len t'ág-čód-pa-na (khyod gnyis nga la chung ma mi len thag chod pa na) if you positively refuse to give me a wife Pth.; *t'ag-čád mi k'yut* (*thag chad mi khyut*) W. I have no right to decide on that point; t'ag-čód-pa byéd-pa (thag chod pa byed pa) to decide, pass sentence, give judgment Mil.; to be sure, decided, certain, ... groṅs-par t'ag-bčád-de (grongs par thag bcad de) (cf. above) as it is quite certain that he has died Mil.; ... yod t'ag-čód (yod thag chod) there are certainly ... Glr.; č'os dar °óṅ t'ag-čód (chos dar 'ong thag chod) it is quite certain that religion will spread Mil.; ltá-bas t'ag-bčád-nas (lta bas thag bcad nas) being immovable in contemplation; with termin.: to know for a certainty, to understand or see clearly, raṅ-sems č'os-skur t'ag-čód-čiṅ (rang sems chos skur thag chod cing) knowing one's own mind to be vain and frail (v. č'os-sku (chos sku) sub sku (sku) 2) Mil.; snáṅ-ba séms-su (snang ba sems su) the visible world as a thought, as imaginary, i.e. as nothing Mil.; t'ag-čód (thag chod) certainty, surety, evidence, °óṅ-kyaṅ t'ag-čód byed dgos ('ong kyang thag chod byed dgos) but one should know it for certain, one must be sure of it Mil.; ltá-ba t'ag-čód-kyi rnál-°byor-pa (lta ba thag chod kyi rnal 'byor pa) you, the ascetic, firm in meditation! Mil. — *t'ag-č'o'-rbe'-č'o'* (*thag cho' rbe' cho'*) C. resolute.
